purpose: Track the current research architecture, the known limitations in version 1, and the next upgrades required for a stronger regime-first Treasury strategy system.
maintenance_rule: Keep the hierarchy fixed unless the research architecture truly changes. Add problems under the affected layer, then attach planned upgrades with priority, rationale, and success metrics.

review_cadence.daily: Log data failures, suspicious posterior moves, broken exports, and source-ingestion problems.
review_cadence.weekly: Review model calibration, player-path quality, and website communication gaps.
review_cadence.monthly: Re-rank upgrade priorities, revise success metrics, and decide whether a version step is required.

principles: Economic regime path is the structural core.
Treasury market is the fastest observation mirror, not the regime itself.
Shock and player behavior are stochastic drivers that bend or accelerate the regime path.
Each improvement should be attached to a specific layer and tested against a clear success metric.

hierarchy.title: Economic Regime Core
hierarchy.role: Define the fixed regime dictionary, admissible transition graph, and the structural ontology of the project.
v1_scope: Daily posterior updates over the same regime graph
known_problems: The regime posterior can collapse too quickly toward one dominant state.
Transition persistence is hand-tuned rather than empirically estimated.
The current regime set is useful but still coarse for a full macro-financial history.

next_upgrades.title: Calibrate posterior persistence and confidence
next_upgrades.problem: Regime probabilities currently become too certain under strong heuristic channel scores.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Add persistence penalties, temperature control, and historical calibration tests so the posterior remains informative rather than overconfident.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: A strategist-facing system must show uncertainty honestly, especially during transition periods.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Dominant regime rarely exceeds 85-90% unless multiple channels align persistently over time.

next_upgrades.title: Extend regime dictionary by historical validation
next_upgrades.problem: The current regime dictionary is interpretable but still compact for historical regime analysis.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Stress-test the six-node map against historical episodes and add sub-regimes only where they improve explanatory power.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: A hiring-manager-facing research system needs a defensible ontology, not ad hoc labels.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Historical event mapping improves without creating an overly fragmented regime space.

hierarchy.title: Treasury Observation Layer
hierarchy.role: Translate public fixed-income data into a rich market state using curve shape, inflation decomposition, funding, issuance, and bond-risk measures.
v1_scope: Daily curve, real yields, breakevens, flies, and Nelson-Siegel factors
Cash-flow-based par-bond duration, convexity, and DV01
known_problems: The term-premium model is more defensible than the old proxy but it is still not an ACM-grade term-structure model.
Funding and repo signals are useful but still public-proxy level rather than true market microstructure depth.
Treasury strategy translation is descriptive and not yet tied to explicit scenario P&L.

next_upgrades.title: Upgrade term premium from VAR-style proxy to affine term-structure model
next_upgrades.problem: The long-end interpretation still depends on a simplified expectation engine.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Implement an ACM-style or state-space affine term-premium estimation workflow.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This is the main quantitative bridge between macro narrative and long-end Treasury interpretation.
next_upgrades.success_metric: 10Y and 30Y term-premium measures become historically stable and economically interpretable across stress periods.

next_upgrades.title: Add explicit Treasury trade and hedge translation
next_upgrades.problem: The site explains the curve well but still stops short of a scenario-by-scenario Treasury trade map.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Add scenario DV01, steepener/flattening exposure, breakeven sensitivity, and representative hedge templates.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: A macro strategist must connect regime logic to risk-taking and risk management.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Each daily note can map the dominant regime path into concrete Treasury positioning language.

next_upgrades.title: Deepen Treasury plumbing signals
next_upgrades.problem: Auction and repo coverage is informative but still incomplete.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Add richer auction internals, dealer statistics, investor allotment splits, and higher-quality repo/funding stress proxies.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This reduces the chance of mistaking market distortion for regime change.
next_upgrades.success_metric: The system more reliably separates supply/funding stress from structural macro repricing.

hierarchy.title: Shock and Evidence Layer
hierarchy.role: Classify shocks ex post from credible sources and turn speeches, reports, and releases into structured evidence claims.
v1_scope: Official Fed and Treasury evidence ingestion
Dominant shock and secondary shock assignment
known_problems: Media evidence still depends on manual summaries rather than a richer workflow.
Claim extraction is structured but still relatively shallow in language interpretation.
Shock severity and persistence are heuristic rather than formally estimated.

next_upgrades.title: Formalize evidence claim scoring by channel and horizon
next_upgrades.problem: Evidence enters the system, but its forward usefulness is not yet rich enough by claim type.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Score claim direction, transmission channel, and persistence separately for D+1, W+1, and M+1.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This tells you which evidence sources deserve trust at each horizon.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Source scorecards clearly distinguish fast tactical sources from slower regime-relevant sources.

next_upgrades.title: Improve shock persistence estimation
next_upgrades.problem: Shock persistence is still attached with a simple heuristic score.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Estimate persistence from realized market follow-through and source-confirmation behavior.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: Shock duration is central to whether a move should reshape the regime path or fade quickly.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Shock half-life tracking becomes more stable across repeated event types.

hierarchy.title: Player Response Layer
hierarchy.role: Infer stochastic action paths for Treasury, Fed, sell side, buy side, and foreign official players across horizons.
v1_scope: Short, mid, and long horizon player probabilities
Structured scenario text in the daily note
known_problems: Player probabilities are currently rule-based rather than estimated with hierarchical statistical models.
Sell-side and buy-side behavior is still captured through broad public proxies.
The model does not yet learn how player-path quality changes across shock categories.

next_upgrades.title: Estimate player actions with hierarchical Bayesian models
next_upgrades.problem: Player behavior is still governed by heuristics tied to feature scores.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Replace hand-tuned action weights with horizon-specific hierarchical multinomial models.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: Player probabilities should become a measurable stochastic process, not just a narrative overlay.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Player-path forecasts improve in calibration and ex-post scoring across multiple horizons.

next_upgrades.title: Separate fast dealer behavior from slower investor behavior more cleanly
next_upgrades.problem: The current player timing logic is conceptually correct but still too broad in the observed proxy layer.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Add dealer and investor timing priors plus proxy-specific adjustment rules.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: Treasury market moves often reflect different reaction speeds by player type.
next_upgrades.success_metric: The system better distinguishes flow-amplification days from slower allocation-driven regime shifts.

hierarchy.title: Statistical and ML Engine
hierarchy.role: Coordinate Bayesian updates, factor extraction, calibration, forecast review, and the supporting econometric logic.
known_problems: The engine combines good ingredients but still lacks a full state-space backbone.
Some scores are still hand-crafted and not estimated jointly.
Model-risk monitoring is not yet explicit enough.

next_upgrades.title: Move from channel heuristics toward a state-space regime engine
next_upgrades.problem: The current posterior is coherent but still partly built from deterministic channel scores.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Introduce a sticky HMM or hidden semi-Markov framework with exogenous shock and Treasury inputs.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This is the natural next step for a more rigorous latent-regime system.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Posterior dynamics become smoother, more persistent, and better calibrated through time.

next_upgrades.title: Add model-risk and diagnostics dashboard
next_upgrades.problem: The system reports outputs, but it does not yet show enough model-risk diagnostics to the user.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Track calibration drift, unstable coefficients, missing-data dependence, and posterior jump diagnostics.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: A serious research process should reveal where the model is fragile.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Research reviews can identify whether a bad call came from data, model structure, or interpretation.

hierarchy.title: Forecast Review and Memory
hierarchy.role: Keep score on regime calls, player scenarios, shock persistence, and evidence-source usefulness across horizons.
known_problems: Forecast memory exists but is still shallow because the archive is young.
Scenario hit-rate scoring is not yet differentiated enough by object type.
There is no explicit error taxonomy attached to each forecast miss.

next_upgrades.title: Add explicit miss taxonomy and forecast attribution
next_upgrades.problem: When the model is wrong, the current review outputs do not fully distinguish whether the mistake came from the regime call, the shock call, or the player weighting.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Log misses as wrong regime, wrong transmission, wrong persistence, wrong player weighting, or exogenous break.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This is how the system improves rather than just accumulates notes.
next_upgrades.success_metric: Each major miss can be tied to a specific architectural weakness and upgrade path.

next_upgrades.title: Make forecast memory visible on the site
next_upgrades.problem: The public site shows current outputs well, but the cumulative learning process is still understated.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Add a dedicated memory view for scenario emergence, fade, and ex-post source performance.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This proves the research process is disciplined rather than one-day narrative writing.
next_upgrades.success_metric: A visitor can understand how the system learns over time without reading raw data files.

hierarchy.title: Website and Communication Layer
hierarchy.role: Turn the research system into a manager-facing presentation that is clean, credible, and easy to scan from a resume link.
v1_scope: Static website payload export from the Python pipeline
known_problems: The site communicates the current system well, but it still needs a stronger explanation of what will improve next.
The visuals are clear, but some analytical layers are still summarized rather than fully visualized.
There is no explicit public roadmap page in version 1 yet.

next_upgrades.title: Expose the version map and research roadmap publicly
next_upgrades.problem: A manager can see the current output, but not the structured path of future improvements.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Publish the version map, hierarchy, known limits, and upgrade queue on the site.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: This signals that the research process is iterative, disciplined, and self-aware.
next_upgrades.success_metric: A visitor can see both what the system already does and where the next improvements will land.

next_upgrades.title: Add richer fixed-income charts and scenario visuals
next_upgrades.problem: The current site is informative, but it still compresses some of the best fixed-income content into text or tables.
next_upgrades.planned_change: Add dedicated curve-factor, term-premium, and player-scenario visualizations.
next_upgrades.why_it_matters: Clear visuals make the research more legible to busy managers and recruiters.
next_upgrades.success_metric: The most important regime and Treasury interpretations are visible without reading long notes.
